title: Migrate docs to .pearls/docs/ and simplify providers config
body:
Atomic migration: move planning docs to `.pearls/docs/`, simplify `madreperla.yaml` providers, and regenerate commands. Per the planning doc, this is one issue because partial states (e.g. moved docs but old providers config) would break command generation.

## Scope

### 1. Update pearls.yaml

Add top-level `docs:` section mapping each doc category to its `.pearls/docs/` path:

```yaml
docs:
  planning: .pearls/docs
  epics: .pearls/docs/epics
  ideas: .pearls/docs/ideas
  headaches: .pearls/docs/headaches
  retros: .pearls/docs/retros
```

### 2. Move doc directories

- `mkdir -p .pearls/docs/retros`
- `git mv docs/planning/epics .pearls/docs/epics` (skip if absent)
- `git mv docs/planning/ideas .pearls/docs/ideas` (skip if absent)
- `git mv docs/planning/headaches .pearls/docs/headaches` (skip if absent)
- Move any standalone `docs/planning/*.md` files to `.pearls/docs/` root (e.g. `gh-extension.md`)
- `touch .pearls/docs/retros/.gitkeep`
- `rmdir docs/planning` if empty

Use `2>/dev/null || true` on `git mv` / `rmdir` so missing dirs don't abort the migration.

### 3. Simplify madreperla.yaml

Replace the existing `providers:` block with:

```yaml
providers:
  issues: pearls
  docs: pearls
```

Leave `docs: [AGENTS.md, README.md, docs/]` (the repo context list) unchanged.

### 4. Regenerate

- `prl configure`
- `madp configure --provider claude`

### 5. Verify

- `grep -r '{docs\.' commands/` must find nothing (no unresolved `{docs.*}` literals in generated commands).

## Acceptance criteria

- `.pearls/docs/` contains `epics/`, `ideas/`, `headaches/`, `retros/` subdirs (retros has `.gitkeep`).
- No `docs/planning/epics/`, `docs/planning/ideas/`, `docs/planning/headaches/` directories remain.
- `docs/planning/` is empty or removed; any standalone `.md` files have moved to `.pearls/docs/`.
- `madreperla.yaml` `providers:` block is simplified to `{issues: pearls, docs: pearls}`.
- `pearls.yaml` has a `docs:` section pointing at `.pearls/docs/` paths.
- Generated commands under `commands/` have fully resolved paths (no `{docs.*}` literals).
- All changes committed and pushed in a single commit.
